LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RITA SCHUMAN, ANDREW SCHUMAN, STEPHANIE SCHUMAN'S MOTION TO HOLD SEPARATE TRIALS TO DECIDE QUESTIONS OF LAW AND TO AJUDICATE EQUITABLE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS RITA, ANDREW, AND STEPHANIE SCHUMAN'S MOTION TO HOLD SEPARATE TRIALS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO MAKING THE SAME REQUEST TO THE TRIAL JUDGE. THE TRIAL JUDGE, NOT A PRE-TRIAL JUDGE, IS IN THE BEST POSITION TO DETERMINE WHETHER THE REQUESTED BIFURCATION ADVANCES JUDICIAL ECONOMY AND FAIR ADJUDICATION. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Hold Separate Trials To Decide Questions Of Law And To Ajudicate Equitable Cause Of Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Allan J. Besbris
Off Calendar Jan-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RITA SCHUMAN, ANDREW SCHUMAN, STEPHANIE SCHUMAN's Motion To Hold Separate Trials To Decide Questions Of Law And To Ajudicate Equitable Cause Of Action.

Plaintiffs Rita, Andrew, and Stephanie Schuman's motion to hold separate trials is denied without prejudice to making the same request to the trial judge. The trial judge, not a pre-trial judge, is in the best position to determine whether the requested bifurcation advances judicial economy and fair adjudication.

Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/HK)
